title: Italy Beat Northern Ireland 1–0 in UEFA Playoff Semifinal (Path A)
detail: Italy defeated Northern Ireland 1–0 at Stadio di Bergamo in the 2026 World Cup UEFA Playoff Path A semifinal. The Azzurri, seeking to avoid a third consecutive World Cup absence after missing out in 2018 and 2022, edged past a Northern Ireland side ranked 69th in the world. Mateo Retegui, Italy's top scorer during qualifying with five goals, was central to Italy's attack. Italy advance to the Path A final on March 31 to face the winner of Wales vs Bosnia and Herzegovina for one of the final four European spots at the 2026 World Cup.

title: Wales Host Bosnia-Herzegovina in UEFA Playoff Semifinal (Path A)
detail: Wales hosted Bosnia and Herzegovina at Cardiff City Stadium in the 2026 World Cup UEFA Playoff Path A semifinal, kicking off at 19:45 UTC. The winner advances to face Italy on March 31 in the Path A final. Wales, managed by Craig Bellamy, finished second in Group J behind Belgium; Bosnia narrowly missed automatic qualification after a late draw against Austria. Harry Wilson (five qualifying goals) led Wales's attack, while veteran Edin Dzeko captained Bosnia at age 40.

title: UEFA Path B Playoff Semifinals: Ukraine vs Sweden, Poland vs Albania
detail: Two UEFA Path B World Cup playoff semifinals took place on March 26 in Valencia and Warsaw. Ukraine faced Sweden in Valencia, while Poland hosted Albania in Warsaw — all kicking off at 19:45 UTC. Sweden, despite a poor qualifying campaign with no wins, entered via Nations League ranking. Sweden's Alexander Isak remained absent with injury, though Viktor Gyökeres (Arsenal) was fit. The Path B winner on March 31 joins Group E alongside the Netherlands, Japan, and Tunisia.

title: UEFA Path C Playoff Semifinals: Turkey vs Romania, Slovakia vs Kosovo
detail: UEFA Path C World Cup playoff semifinals were held on March 26. Turkey (featuring Real Madrid's Arda Güler, Juventus winger Kenan Yildiz, and Inter Milan's Hakan Çalhanoglu) hosted Romania in Istanbul at 17:00 UTC — the earliest kickoff of the day. Slovakia then hosted Kosovo in Bratislava at 19:45 UTC. Kosovo, ranked 79th and the lowest-seeded team in the European playoffs, faced a Slovakia side that had conceded no goals in all three home qualifying matches. The Path C winner joins Group C with host USA, Paraguay, and Australia.

title: UEFA Path D Playoff Semifinals: Denmark vs North Macedonia, Czechia vs Republic of Ireland
detail: UEFA Path D World Cup playoff semifinals took place on March 26, with Denmark hosting North Macedonia in Copenhagen and Czechia hosting the Republic of Ireland in Prague, both at 19:45 UTC. Denmark, anchored by Christian Eriksen and Rasmus Højlund, missed automatic qualification by a single point despite a dominant campaign, with Kasper Schmeichel unavailable through injury. The Path D winner joins Group A with co-host Mexico, South Africa, and South Korea.

title: Bolivia vs Suriname: Intercontinental Playoff Semifinal in Monterrey
detail: Bolivia faced Suriname in the FIFA World Cup 2026 intercontinental play-off tournament semifinal at Estadio BBVA in Monterrey, Mexico, kicking off at 23:00 UTC. The winner advances to face Iraq in the Pathway 2 final on March 31 for one of two remaining World Cup berths. Bolivia — whose miraculous 1-0 win over Brazil was a highlight of CONMEBOL qualifying — relied heavily on striker Miguel Terceros (seven qualifying goals) without the altitude advantage of La Paz. Suriname, bolstered by Dutch-born talent including Leeds United's Joël Piroe, were chasing their first-ever World Cup appearance. The Pathway 2 winner enters Group I with France, Senegal, and Norway.

title: New Caledonia vs Jamaica: Intercontinental Playoff Semifinal in Guadalajara
detail: New Caledonia and Jamaica met in the FIFA World Cup 2026 intercontinental play-off tournament Pathway 1 semifinal at Estadio Guadalajara (Estadio Akron) in Guadalajara, Mexico. The winner advances to face DR Congo — seeded directly to the final as the highest-ranked team in Pathway 1 — on March 31 for one of two final World Cup berths. New Caledonia qualified via OFC after falling 3-0 to New Zealand in the OFC final, while Jamaica entered as one of the best third-place finishers from CONCACAF qualifying with 11 points from Group B. Jamaica were the strong favourites against Oceania's representatives.
